rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in component type scopes
Place implementation annexes in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings to existing outputs or auxiliary variables
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations and comparisons
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope and visibility rules when inserting annex blocks
Preserve existing component and connection structure during model fusion
Use valid AGREE syntax patterns for contracts and equations
Avoid assumptions and guarantees that reference implementation-only features in type scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for AGREE contract blocks
Use `assign` only for implementation-level bindings to visible outputs
Use `gua` for guarantees and `ass` for assumptions in AGREE contracts
Use `eq` for equation declarations in AGREE contracts
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not use opaque Boolean data in range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names (use int, real, bool instead)
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assign placement in type scopes
